nodes.name: Research Intake Webhook
nodes.notes:
POST /trading/research-intake
Header Auth: X-Webhook-Secret
Accepts { content: string, source?: string }
This is the primary manual intake — paste NotebookLM research briefs here.

nodes.name: Validate Payload
nodes.notes: Rejects empty or too-short payloads. Content must be >50 chars to contain anything useful.

nodes.name: Normalize Input
nodes.notes: Strips NotebookLM footnotes, source references, zero-width characters, and normalizes whitespace.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 2048,
  "system": "You are a trading signal extraction engine for a wheel strategy (cash-secured puts, covered calls) portfolio.

Given a research brief, extract every actionable trading signal.

For each signal found, output:
- ticker: stock symbol (uppercase, e.g. AAPL)
- direction: "bullish" | "bearish" | "neutral"
- thesis: one-sentence rationale (max 120 chars)
- source_type: "congressional" | "flow" | "fundamental" | "technical" | "macro" | "catalyst"
- catalysts: array of upcoming events relevant to this signal
- risk_factors: array of risk items
- sector: GICS sector name
- congressional_refs: array of politician names if any congressional trading mentioned (empty array if none)
- timeframe: "short" (<30d) | "medium" (30-90d) | "long" (>90d)

Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No markdown fences, no explanation, no preamble.
Format: { "signals": [ { ... } ] }
If no actionable signals found: { "signals": [] }",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Research brief content:

" + $json.normalized_content + "

Date context: " + $json.intake_date
    }
  ]
}) }}

nodes.name: Claude — Extract Signals
nodes.notes:
First Claude pass: extracts structured trading signals from raw research text.
Uses claude-sonnet-4-20250514 for speed/cost balance.
Timeout 30s. Auth via Header Auth credential (x-api-key).

nodes.name: Parse Extraction
nodes.notes: Safe JSON parse with fallback. Strips markdown fences if Claude includes them despite instructions.

nodes.name: Has Signals?
nodes.notes: Routes: signals found → scoring pipeline. No signals or error → skip to archive + log.

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 2048,
  "system": "You are a trading conviction scorer for a wheel strategy (cash-secured puts, covered calls) portfolio.

Score each signal using this exact framework:

Signal Strength Score (1-10):
+3  Committee member trading in their committee's sector
+2  Trade size > $100K or major institutional flow
+2  Multiple independent sources confirming same thesis
+1  Party-line consensus (bipartisan buying)
+1  Trade made before known catalyst
+1  Stock is in S&P 500 (liquid, good options market)
+1  IV Rank likely > 30 (rich premiums for selling)
-2  Stale data (> 30 days old or vague timing)
-1  Single unconfirmed source
-1  Earnings within 14 days (assignment risk for wheel)

Base score is 3. Add/subtract from there. Clamp to 1-10 range.

For each signal, also assess:
- wheel_eligible: true if stock has liquid weekly/monthly options, market cap > $10B, and fundamentally sound enough to own at a discount
- suggested_strategy: "CSP" (bullish, sell cash-secured put) | "CC" (already own, sell covered call) | "watch" (interesting but wait for better entry) | "avoid" (bearish or too risky)
- premium_environment: "rich" (high IV, fat premiums) | "fair" (normal IV) | "thin" (low IV, not worth selling)

Respond ONLY with valid JSON. No markdown fences, no explanation.
Format: { "scored_signals": [ { ...all_original_fields, "conviction": N, "wheel_eligible": bool, "suggested_strategy": "...", "premium_environment": "..." } ] }",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Score these extracted trading signals:
" + JSON.stringify($json.signals)
    }
  ]
}) }}

nodes.name: Claude — Score Signals
nodes.notes:
Second Claude pass: applies Signal Strength framework scoring.
Scores 1-10 conviction, wheel eligibility, strategy suggestion, premium environment.
Same auth credential as Extract node.

nodes.name: Parse Scoring
nodes.notes: Parse scored signals. Graceful degradation: if scoring fails, passes through original signals with default base score of 3 so data is never lost.

nodes.name: Prep Dedup Query
nodes.notes: Extracts unique ticker list for the Supabase dedup lookup.

parameters.query: SELECT ticker, thesis, conviction, status FROM trading_signals WHERE ticker = ANY(string_to_array('{{ $json.ticker_filter }}', ',')) AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

nodes.name: Dedup Check — Supabase
nodes.notes:
Checks for existing signals on the same tickers within the last 7 days.
Auth: use a Header Auth credential with name=Authorization, value=Bearer {{SERVICE_KEY}}.
The apikey header uses the anon key.

nodes.name: Merge & Validate
nodes.notes:
Deduplicates against existing signals, validates required fields, normalizes data types for Supabase.
JSONB fields (catalysts, risk_factors, congressional_refs) are JSON.stringified for the REST API.
Flags is_update for upsert logic.

parameters.query: ={{ 'INSERT INTO trading_signals (ticker, direction, thesis, source_type, catalysts, risk_factors, sector, congressional_refs, timeframe, conviction, wheel_eligible, suggested_strategy, premium_environment) VALUES ' + $json.signals.filter(s => !s._is_update).map(s => '(' + [JSON.stringify(s.ticker),JSON.stringify(s.direction),JSON.stringify(s.thesis),JSON.stringify(s.source_type)].join(',') + ',' + [JSON.stringify(JSON.stringify(s.catalysts||[])),JSON.stringify(JSON.stringify(s.risk_factors||[])),JSON.stringify(JSON.stringify(s.congressional_refs||[]))].join(',') + '::jsonb,' + [JSON.stringify(s.sector||''),JSON.stringify(s.timeframe||'')].join(',') + ',' + s.conviction + ',' + (s.wheel_eligible?'true':'false') + ',' + [JSON.stringify(s.suggested_strategy||''),JSON.stringify(s.premium_environment||'')].join(',') + ')').join(',') + ' ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING' }}

nodes.name: Upsert Signals — Supabase
nodes.notes:
Upserts all validated signals into trading_signals table.
Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates handles the UNIQUE constraint on (ticker, thesis, created_at::date).
Auth: same Header Auth credential as dedup (Authorization: Bearer SERVICE_KEY).

parameters.query: ={{ "INSERT INTO research_briefs (content, source, signal_count, top_conviction, tickers_mentioned) VALUES ('" + $('Normalize Input').first().json.normalized_content.replace(/'/g,"''") + "','" + ($('Normalize Input').first().json.source||'notebooklm') + "'," + ($('Merge & Validate').first().json.total||0) + "," + (($('Merge & Validate').first().json.high_conviction||[]).reduce((m,s)=>Math.max(m,s.conviction||0),0)) + ",'" + JSON.stringify(($('Merge & Validate').first().json.tickers_processed||[])).replace(/'/g,"''") + "'::jsonb) RETURNING id" }}

nodes.name: Archive Brief — Supabase
nodes.notes:
Archives the raw research brief with metadata (signal count, top conviction, tickers mentioned).
Used for historical analysis and feedback loop tuning.

parameters.query: ={{ "INSERT INTO workflow_runs (workflow_name, status, signals_extracted, signals_upserted, duration_ms) VALUES ('notebooklm-trading-bridge', 'success', " + ($('Merge & Validate').first().json.total||0) + ", " + ($('Merge & Validate').first().json.new_count||0) + ", " + (Date.now() - new Date($('Normalize Input').first().json.intake_timestamp).getTime()) + ")" }}

nodes.name: Log Run — Supabase
nodes.notes: Audit trail: logs workflow execution status, signal counts, duration, and input hash for dedup tracking.

nodes.name: High Conviction?
nodes.notes: Routes high-conviction signals (≥7) to Slack alert. If none, skips straight to response.

nodes.name: Slack Alert — High Conviction
nodes.notes:
Sends formatted Slack message for signals scoring ≥7 conviction.
Includes ticker, score, strategy, thesis, sector, premium environment, and wheel eligibility.
Optional: replace with SMS via Twilio node if preferred.

nodes.name: Build Response
nodes.notes: Constructs the final webhook response payload with signal summary, high-conviction highlights, and processing metadata.

nodes.name: No Signals Response
nodes.notes: Handles the case where no signals were extracted (empty research or extraction error). Still returns a clean response.

parameters.query: ={{ "INSERT INTO research_briefs (content, source, signal_count, top_conviction, tickers_mentioned) VALUES ('" + $('Normalize Input').first().json.normalized_content.replace(/'/g,"''") + "','" + ($('Normalize Input').first().json.source||'notebooklm') + "'," + ($('Merge & Validate').first().json.total||0) + "," + (($('Merge & Validate').first().json.high_conviction||[]).reduce((m,s)=>Math.max(m,s.conviction||0),0)) + ",'" + JSON.stringify(($('Merge & Validate').first().json.tickers_processed||[])).replace(/'/g,"''") + "'::jsonb) RETURNING id" }}

nodes.name: Archive Brief (No Signals)
nodes.notes: Archives the research brief even when no signals were found — useful for tuning extraction prompts later.

parameters.query: ={{ "INSERT INTO workflow_runs (workflow_name, status, signals_extracted, signals_upserted, duration_ms) VALUES ('notebooklm-trading-bridge', 'success', " + ($('Merge & Validate').first().json.total||0) + ", " + ($('Merge & Validate').first().json.new_count||0) + ", " + (Date.now() - new Date($('Normalize Input').first().json.intake_timestamp).getTime()) + ")" }}

nodes.name: Log Run (No Signals)
nodes.notes: Logs the workflow run even when no signals found or an error occurred.

nodes.name: Reject Response
nodes.notes: Returns 200 with rejection message when payload validation fails. Webhook still responds cleanly.
